steps.title: Target persuasion evidence
steps.content: The claim is that Marianne can persuade others that her artistic judgments are right. The strongest quotation must show her overcoming disagreement, not merely sharing interests or enjoying conversation.

steps.title: Reject shared-interest quotes
steps.content: Choice A shows Marianne approving Willoughby's existing interest in music and dancing and gaining his attention. Choice C shows she speaks freely about favorite amusements. Choice D shows mutual agreement about dancing and music before books are discussed. These details show interest or similarity, not persuasion.

steps.title: Match the arguments phrase
steps.content: The remaining quotation says any difference or objection lasted only until Marianne's arguments could be displayed. That wording shows her actively overcoming Willoughby's objections about artistic judgments, which is the claim the quotation must illustrate. The answer is B.
